title: geum ciliatum var. griseum
intro: Geum ciliatum var. griseum is a perennial plant species, commonly known as gray avens or pale avens, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Geum ciliatum var. griseum has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, due to its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the roots of the plant are typically dried and used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
